failure_reason: Moved BML/Hati compiled method calls from name-only lookup to arity-selected overload resolution in the method environment. The focused proof returns 19 and verifies Pick(), Pick(int), Pick(int,int), plus the wrong-arity BML-HATI-UNSUPPORTED call/arity path; binary mode also returns 19. Floor proof returns 26; existing method-call, multi-arg, and class/template/overload proofs remain 19, 20, and 23; bml-compiler-runtime returns 131012; compiler proof returns 39. Pre-rebase full Form validation returned fourth arm: 138 band(s) four-way, 560 ok, and 0 divergent. After rebase, upstream carries the record-carrier floor at 139 four-way bands and this slice adds one passing overload proof sample, so fourth-arm-bands.txt records 139 four-way bands and 560 total passing. Two post-rebase full-suite retries were terminated by the host during fourth-arm table flattening before any divergence summary was emitted. The metal-plane audit passes macOS arm64 Mach-O and Android arm64 ELF clang-oracle assembly/object receipts plus Form-native JIT/lowered/native hardware-lane recipe proofs. This is not a 100 percent Form-native object-emitter claim; the new BML/Hati proof is 3-kernel only while fourth-arm mutable record admission and full native asm emission remain named follow-on work.
